channel.title: NYT > Business > Energy & Environment
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
item.description: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: In response to Iranian threats, commercial shipping has come to a standstill in the Gulf, and oil prices have spiked.

item.title: Higher Oil Prices Could Put the Fed in a Bind as Labor Market Softens
item.description: Investors now expect that the Fed will delay a rate cut until September instead of July, as they had before the war in Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: Economists expect energy-related price pressures to push inflation up to some degree in the coming months.

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Pressure Airlines and Truckers
item.description: The price of jet fuel and diesel has surged since the war in Iran began, which could force airlines and trucking companies to pass on higher costs to their customers.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi, Emmett Lindner and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: The cost of jet fuel has reached record highs since the U.S.-led war with Iran began.

item.title: U.S. Solar Installations Fell in 2025 as Trump Attacked Clean Energy
item.description: More solar energy was added to U.S. grids than any other technology, but the amount installed fell by 14 percent, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Mason Trinca for The New York Times
item.media:description: A solar field in Riesel, Texas, in 2023. Solar power is projected to account for just over half of the new power projects that will be added this year.

item.title: Oil Prices Will Remain High as Iran War Continues
item.description: While officials look for ways to ease oil shocks, experts say higher prices will likely persist until traffic through the Strait of Hormuz returns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Black smoke filled the skies above Tehran on Sunday morning after Israeli airstrikes struck energy facilities overnight.

item.title: G7 Countries Hold Off on Releasing Emergency Oil Supplies
item.description: Finance ministers for the seven industrialized countries met on Monday and said they would consider releasing oil from reserves but were not ready to do so now.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Smoke rose from a refinery operated by Bapco Energies, Bahrain’s state-owned energy company, on Monday. Global oil prices have spiked since the U.S.-led war with Iran began.

item.title: ‘The Old Game Is Back’: Oil Is a Potent Geopolitical Tool Again
item.description: The war in the Middle East has sent prices soaring, showing how much the world continues to depend on reliable supplies of oil and gas.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Damian Dovarganes/Associated Press
item.media:description: An oil tanker off the coast of El Segundo, Calif., last week. The United States is the world’s biggest producer of oil and natural gas but is still feeling the effects of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Prices Spike to Over $110 a Barrel, Highest Since Pandemic
item.description: The jump was a sign of growing concern that the war in the Middle East will take a toll on energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: The price of a gallon of regular gasoline has climbed about 16 percent since the United States and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28.

item.title: Defense Without U.S. Help Is a Live Topic for Canada, Japan and Australia
item.description: The leaders of Japan and Canada are making a unified front on defense cooperation as President Trump raises the pressure over military spending.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving in Tokyo on Friday as part of a tour that also included Australia.

item.title: Nature Report, Killed by Trump, Is Released Independently
item.description: A draft assessment of the health of nature in the United States is grim but shot through with bright spots and possibility.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn
item.category: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering and Medicine
item.media:credit: David Robert Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A dried part of the Arkansas River in Garden City, Kan.

item.title: Should New York City Burn Its Parks? This Scientist Thinks So.
item.description: An unusual outbreak of wildfires in city parks gave scientists a chance to study these rare events. Now they’re coming to different conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Karsten Moran
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s High-Profile Oil and Gas Lease Sale in Alaska Has No Takers
item.description: No companies bid for the chance to drill in more than 1 million acres of water off Alaska’s Cook Inlet, the Interior Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.category: Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, Regulation and Enforcement
item.media:credit: Nathaniel Wilder for The New York Times
item.media:description: An oil platform in the Cook Inlet last year.

item.title: Why Data Center Firms Are Working With Trump on Energy Costs
item.description: The White House has floated a new plan to try to ease voter concerns over the A.I. boom’s effect on electric bills. But it won’t be easy to put into practice.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.media:credit: Christie Hemm Klok for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Google data center. Large data centers can use as much electricity as a small city.

item.title: Liquid Natural Gas Exporters Like Shell and ExxonMobil Could See Big Profits From War
item.description: Prices for liquefied natural gas have shot up in recent days, which could bring bigger profits for European and U.S. energy companies.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas production facility in Qatar this week. Companies like Shell, TotalEnergies and ExxonMobil could benefit from the war.

item.title: TerraPower Nuclear Reactor in Wyoming Gets Federal Permit
item.description: TerraPower’s project in Wyoming, which uses novel technology, is the first new commercial reactor to receive federal approval in nearly a decade.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.media:credit: Benjamin Rasmussen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Politicians and executives, including Bill Gates, shoveled dirt during the groundbreaking for TerraPower’s Natrium nuclear power plant in Kemmerer, Wyo.

item.title: Sea Levels Are Already Higher Than Many Scientists Think, New Study Shows
item.description: Researchers found that a majority of studies on coastal sea levels underestimated how high water levels are, and hundreds of millions of people are closer to peril than previously thought.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Mira Rojanasakul
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Juan Barreto/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A stretch of coast in Suriname, one of many places affected by sea level rise and erosion caused by climate change

item.title: Kristi Noem Review Delayed FEMA Aid by Weeks, Senate Finds
item.description: A report by Senate Democrats found that scrutiny of large FEMA expenditures slowed housing and employment assistance for victims of fires and storms.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Al Drago/Getty Images
item.media:description: Kristi Noem, the homeland security secretary, at the Federal Emergency Management Agency headquarters in Washington in January.

item.title: Oil and Natural Gas Prices Keep Climbing as Iran Attacks Escalate
item.description: Energy prices continued to rise on Tuesday as attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran intensified, along with the country’s response.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Drivers queuing at a gas station in Los Angeles on Monday. The price of gas was up by about 11 cents a gallon on Tuesday morning.

item.title: Trump Bets That ‘Energy Dominance’ Will Make War in Iran Less Costly
item.description: So far, the energy price shocks have been relatively contained. But a prolonged conflict in the Middle East carries enormous economic risks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer, Lisa Friedman and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: J. Emilio Flores for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kern River Oil fields near Bakersfield, Calif.

item.title: Scientists Decry ‘Political Attack’ on the Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence
item.description: More than two dozen contributors to the manual criticized the deletion of a chapter on climate science by the Federal Judicial Center.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick
item.category: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering and Medicine
item.media:credit: Andrew Kelly/Reuters
item.media:description: The Thurgood Marshall Federal Judiciary Building where the Federal Judicial Center is located.
